_meta.keyKnowledge: We are learning to describe how a text reflects the time and place in which it was created.

_source.w: We are learning to describe how a text reflects the time and place in which it was created.
y: Understanding context helps us interpret texts more accurately and appreciate why they were written the way they were.
Texts are products of their time — recognising this prevents us from judging them only by modern standards.
This analytical skill deepens our understanding of history, culture and social change.
eg: We identify language in an older text that reflects the social norms of its time.
We discuss how a text's setting and themes reflect the author's historical context.
We compare two texts from different eras and discuss what changed and what remained the same.
We research the historical context of a text and explain how it influenced the content.
We discuss how a modern reader might interpret a text differently from its original audience.
We analyse how cultural context shapes the values and perspectives presented in a text.
